w:r.w:t: PostgreSQL Schema | Stored Procedures | Authentication | Indexing
The following stack is recommended for LPFMS backend. It is chosen for scalability, developer productivity, and clean integration with the React/Vite frontend.
Follow this modular structure. Each module has its own routes, controllers, services, and types. Business logic lives only in services — never in controllers or routes.
LPFMS uses a dual-token authentication strategy. This is industry standard for SPAs and React frontends.
Use refresh token to get new access token
Full access — all modules, user management, close day, void entries
All modules read/write except user management and void
Cash flow entries, sales, read-only reports
Minimum 8 characters, must include uppercase, lowercase, number
Passwords hashed with bcrypt, cost factor 12
Failed login attempts tracked — account locked after 5 failures for 30 minutes
Password reset via OTP sent to registered email
export const authenticate = async (req, res, next) => {
return res.status(401).json({ error: 'Invalid or expired token' });
Complete schema with all tables, relationships, constraints, and comments. This is the single source of truth for the database.
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
-- positive = we owe vendor, negative = vendor owes us
is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
-- positive = customer owes us, negative = we owe customer
is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
-- Each row = one purchase batch from one vendor.
-- Dal Mash from Vendor A ≠ Dal Mash from Vendor B.
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
batch_ref VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL, -- e.g. BATCH-20260301-001
item_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, -- e.g. Dal Mash
vendor_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES vendors(id),
quantity_kg NUMERIC(12,3) NOT NULL, -- original quantity
remaining_qty_kg NUMERIC(12,3) NOT NULL, -- auto-decremented on sale
-- set when batch came from advance delivery
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
batch_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES inventory_batches(id),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
sale_ref VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL, -- e.g. SALE-20260301-001
customer_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES customers(id),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
-- Line items for each sale (multiple items per sale)
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
sale_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES sales(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
batch_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES inventory_batches(id),
-- Contract with vendor for future delivery
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
vendor_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES vendors(id),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
booking_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES advance_bookings(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
grade VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL CHECK (grade IN ('A+','A','B','C')),
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
booking_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES advance_bookings(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
paid_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
closing_balance NUMERIC(15,2), -- set when day is closed
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
-- Individual cash entries during the day
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
cash_day_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES cash_days(id),
entry_type VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL CHECK (entry_type IN ('IN','OUT')),
-- OUT: SALARY, TRANSPORT, UTILITIES, VENDOR_PAYMENT, MISC
entry_time TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
is_locked BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE
-- becomes TRUE when day is closed
-- Full double-entry ledger for each vendor
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
vendor_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES vendors(id),
-- debit = payment made to vendor (reduces our liability)
-- credit = purchase from vendor (increases our liability)
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
-- Full double-entry ledger for each customer
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
customer_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES customers(id),
-- debit = sale to customer (increases what they owe us)
-- credit = payment received (reduces what they owe us)
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
Indexes are critical for performance. Below is the complete indexing plan. Indexes are added on all foreign keys, all frequently filtered columns, and all columns used in ORDER BY.
CREATE INDEX idx_inventory_date ON inventory_batches(purchase_date DESC);
-- Composite: filter by item + grade together (most common query)
CREATE INDEX idx_inventory_item_grade ON inventory_batches(item_name, grade);
CREATE INDEX idx_movements_date ON inventory_movements(created_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_vendor_ledger_date ON vendor_ledger(entry_date DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_customer_ledger_date ON customer_ledger(entry_date DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_sales_date ON sales(sale_date DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_cash_days_date ON cash_days(business_date DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_cash_entries_time ON cash_entries(entry_time DESC);
All critical business operations run as atomic PostgreSQL stored procedures. This ensures data consistency — if any step fails, the entire transaction rolls back.
This is the most critical procedure. It creates a sale, deducts inventory, updates the customer ledger, and records cash flow — all in one atomic transaction.
p_cash_day_id UUID -- link to today's cash register
FOR v_item IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(p_items) LOOP
INSERT INTO sales(sale_ref, customer_id, sale_date, total_amount,
FOR v_item IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(p_items) LOOP
INSERT INTO sale_items(sale_id, batch_id, quantity_kg, sale_price_per_kg)
SELECT current_balance INTO v_cust_balance FROM customers
-- Add to customer ledger (debit = they owe us more)
UPDATE customers SET current_balance = current_balance + v_total,
-- If cash was paid, record in cash register
IF p_amount_paid > 0 AND p_cash_day_id IS NOT NULL THEN
UPDATE cash_days SET total_cash_in = total_cash_in + p_amount_paid
RETURNS NUMERIC AS $$ -- returns closing balance
WHERE id = p_cash_day_id AND status = 'OPEN') THEN
RAISE EXCEPTION 'Day is already closed or does not exist';
-- Lock all entries for this day
-- Create inventory batch for each booking item
SELECT * FROM booking_items WHERE booking_id = p_booking_id
SET status = 'DELIVERED', delivered_at = NOW(), updated_at = NOW()
SELECT * INTO v_cheque FROM cheques WHERE id = p_cheque_id;
RAISE EXCEPTION 'Only PENDING cheques can be bounced';
-- Re-add to vendor ledger (debt is back)
All endpoints are prefixed with /api/v1. All protected routes require Authorization: Bearer <token> header.
Project setup, PostgreSQL + Prisma, Auth (JWT + bcrypt, roles)
— End of LPFMS Backend Implementation Plan —
